IRS990.ActivityOrMissionDesc: WAKELAND HOUSING AND DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION IS A CALIFORNIA NOT-FOR-PROFIT CORPORATION WHOSE MISSION IS TO DEVELOP AND OPERATE QUALITY AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECTS WITH RESIDENT-EDUCATION PROGRAMS FOR LOW-INCOME FAMILIES.
IRS990.MissionDesc: WAKELAND HOUSING AND DEVELOPMENT CORPORATION IS A CALIFORNIA NOT-FOR-PROFIT CORPORATION WHOSE MISSION IS TO DEVELOP AND OPERATE QUALITY AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECTS WITH RESIDENT-EDUCATION PROGRAMS FOR LOW-INCOME FAMILIES.
IRS990.Desc: RENTAL EXPENSES: THE CORPORATION OWNS AND OPERATES A MOBILE HOME PARK, POINSETTIA PARKS, LLC AND AFFORDABLE HOUSING PROJECTS TO PROVIDE AFFORDABLE HOUSING TO LOW AND MODERATE INCOME FAMILIES INCLUDING SENIORS, AND ALSO PROVIDES RESIDENT EDUCATION PROGRAMS TO THE TENANTS. THESE EXPENSES ARE COMPRISED OF OPERATIONAL EXPENSES SUCH AS UTILITIES, OPERATIONS AND MAINTENANCE, AND TAXES AND INSURANCE.

ProgSrvcAccomActy2Grp.Desc: DEVELOPMENT: THE CORPORATION'S MISSION IS TO DEVELOP AFFORDABLE HOUSING USING TWO METHODS: (1) ACQUISITION AND REHABILITATION OF EXISTING MULTIFAMILY RESIDENTIAL HOUSING; AND (2) ACQUISITION AND ENTITLEMENT OF LAND AND CONSTRUCTION OF NEW BUILDINGS.

ProgSrvcAccomActy3Grp.Desc: RESIDENT SERVICES: THE RESIDENT SERVICES DEPARTMENT IS RESPONSIBLE FOR ALL ONSITE DAY-TO-DAY RESIDENT SERVICES PROGRAM IMPLEMENTATION. THE DEPARTMENT PROVIDES INITIAL SET UP, CONDUCTS ONGOING ASSESSMENTS OF COMMUNITY'S PROGRAM NEEDS, AND DEVELOPS COMMUNITY PARTNERS WHILE FACILITATING COMPREHENSIVE PROGRAMS FOR YOUTH, ADULTS AND SENIORS.

ProgSrvcAccomActyOtherGrp.Desc: ASSET MANAGEMENT: THE ASSET MANAGEMENT DEPARTMENT ADMINISTERS ALL PLACED IN SERVICE REAL ESTATE IN THE CORPORATION'S DIVERSE PORTFOLIO, WHICH CONSISTS OF 6,341 HOUSING UNITS. THE DEPARTMENT OVERSEES ALL PROPERTY TAX ABATEMENT ACTIVITIES, MONITORS REGULATORY MANDATES, HIRES PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANIES, PROVIDES PROPERTY MANAGEMENT OVERSIGHT, AND DEVELOPS OPERATING BUDGETS AND CAPITAL IMPROVEMENT PLANS.

OtherRevenueMiscGrp.Desc: GAIN ON STATE CERTIFIED TAX CREDI

SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: THE CORPORATION IS A PUBLIC CHARITY AND IS EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAXES UNDER SECTION 501(C)(3) OF THE INTERNAL REVENUE CODE AND SECTION 23701(D) OF THE CALIFORNIA REVENUE AND TAXATION CODE. THE CORPORATION BELIEVES THAT IT HAS APPROPRIATE SUPPORT FOR ANY TAX POSITIONS TAKEN, AND AS SUCH, DOES NOT HAVE ANY UNCERTAIN TAX POSITIONS THAT ARE MATERIAL TO THE FINANCIAL STATEMENTS. THE CORPORATION IS NOT A PRIVATE FOUNDATION. THE CORPORATION'S RETURN OF ORGANIZATION EXEMPT FROM INCOME TAX FOR THE YEARS ENDED JUNE 30, 2023, 2022, 2021, AND 2020 ARE SUBJECT TO EXAMINATION BY THE INTERNAL REVENUE SERVICE AND STATE TAXING AUTHORITIES, GENERALLY THE THREE TO FOUR YEARS AFTER THE RETURNS WERE FILED.
PREPAID RENT: $-2,748 REALIZED GAIN (LOSS) ON INVESTMENTS: $-129,046

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION A, LINE 3
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: DESCRIPTION OF DELEGATED DUTIES TO MANAGEMENT COMPANY THE ORGANIZATION HAS CONTRACTED WITH UNAFFILIATED PROPERTY MANAGEMENT COMPANIES TO MANAGE THE DAILY OPERATIONS OF ALL THE APARTMENT COMPLEXES.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 11B
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: FORM 990 REVIEW PROCESS THE 990 IS REVIEWED AND APPROVED BY THE AUDIT COMMITTEE. THEN THE 990 IS DISTRIBUTED TO THE BOARD OF DIRECTORS.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 12C
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: EXPLANATION OF MONITORING AND ENFORCEMENT OF CONFLICTS IF THERE WERE ANY CONFLICTS WITH A BOARD MEMBER, THEY ARE ASKED TO RECUSE THEMSELVES WITH ANY MATTERS ON THE CONFLICT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION B, LINE 15
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: COMPENSATION REVIEW & APPROVAL PROCESS - OFFICERS & KEY EMPLOYEES THE ORGANIZATION USES INFORMATION FROM SALARY STUDIES TO DETERMINE COMPENSATION FOR THE CEO, PRESIDENT, KEY EMPLOYEES AND TOP MANAGEMENT.

SupplementalInformationDetail.FormAndLineReferenceDesc: FORM 990, PART VI, SECTION C, LINE 19
SupplementalInformationDetail.ExplanationTxt: OTHER ORGANIZATION DOCUMENTS PUBLICLY AVAILABLE GOVERNING DOCUMENTS, POLICIES AND FINANCIAL STATEMENTS ARE AVAILABLE UPON REQUEST.

DEVELOPMENT EXPENSES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 275,882. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 275,882. REPAIRS AND MAINTENANCE: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 246,692. TOTAL EXPENSES 226,597. RENT EXPENSES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 136,901. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 19,576. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 1,030. TOTAL EXPENSES 157,507. MANAGEMENT FEES: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 147,494. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 147,494. PROPERTY TAX: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 68,990. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 68,990. STATE TAX: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 53,051. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 760. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 40. TOTAL EXPENSES 53,851. OTHER TAX: PROGRAM SERVICE EXPENSES 9,532. MANAGEMENT AND GENERAL EXPENSES 0. FUNDRAISING EXPENSES 0. TOTAL EXPENSES 9,532.
